name: Hate.
description:
"The scourge of everything."

Hate. It circulates in all living beings. It is the only potent essence capable of filling a hollow shell. It is able to be denied. It is able to be suppressed. But not fully cleansed. It appears in many forms on a day-to-day basis. Frustration. Impatience. Impulsiveness. Rudeness.

The kindhearted would try to hide it. Try to stow away it all. Until it all bottles up into a ticking time bomb. Although the efforts are clear, it is in vain. What happens when one spills it all out? When they decide to drop their act? When they decide to harm those around them, and push it all away?

Hate. Hate. Hate. It circulates in all.

But, love powers through all. Not the chase for a quick happy ending, or an unhealthy infatuation with someone. All forms of love assist with suppressing these feelings of hate. The feeling of knowing that every waking moment, there is somebody who cares. To be able to endure hardship, and still be alive, is an accomplishment to praise. Love yourself. Love others. care for them. Show them gratitude. Show them appreciation. No gesture needs to be too large for one to execute. A simple "Thank you." or "Love you." works. Even your presence alone is the greatest gift one could ask for. Stay safe. Learn to appreciate your own efforts. Reflect on all actions, both positive and negative. Negative experiences are learning experiences, to shape and mold one into the best version of themself, after all.

Anyways, thanks for 3000 followers. I have not much else to say. No, this post isn't related to any reviews under my bots whatsoever, I just had to get this thought train out. Yes, I'm still leaving. To be hateful is naught to be unique. Each and every one of you are unique. Your talents. Your goals. Your moral values. Your social dynamics. If you want my two cents on it, what makes somebody on this site unique is making a bot out of a character that has never been on JAI before. It may not be perfect, but that’s why the edit button exists. You won’t get it perfect the first time, and you shouldn’t expect to.
